content: Hi, I'm Ezra. I just got back from a trip to Japan and I'm still on a high from the experience. I spent 2 weeks traveling through Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka.
Welcome back, Ezra! Japan is such a fascinating country. What were some of the highlights of your trip?
Definitely the hiking I did in the Japanese Alps. The scenery was stunning. And I also got to visit some amazing temples and shrines, which was really interesting for me as a photographer.
That sounds amazing. The Japanese Alps are so beautiful. And photography is a great way to capture the beauty of a place. Did you get any great shots during your trip?
Yes, I did. I took over 1,000 photos during my trip, and I'm still sorting through them. I also got to try out my new drone, which was a lot of fun.
That's terrific! Drones can be such a great way to capture unique perspectives. Did you have a favorite city or town that you visited?
I think my favorite was Kyoto. The food was amazing and the people were so friendly. I ended up spending 5 days there and even got to visit the Fushimi Inari shrine on April 20th.

Kyoto is a beautiful city. The temples and gardens are so serene. And visiting the Fushimi Inari shrine must have been a powerful experience. How did you find the food in Japan compared to what you're used to in the US?

I loved it. I'm a big fan of sushi and ramen, and I got to try all sorts of local specialties. I even took a cooking class in Tokyo where I learned how to make traditional Japanese dishes like tonkatsu.
That sounds delicious. Cooking classes can be such a great way to learn about a culture. Did you have a favorite dish that you tried during your trip?
Definitely the tonkatsu. I've already made it a few times since I got back. And I'm thinking of entering a cooking competition in my hometown of Portland next year.
That's great! It's always exciting to share your passions with others. Good luck with the competition, and I hope you get to share your love of cooking with the community.

Thanks, I'm looking forward to it. And I'm also thinking of starting a blog to write about my travel experiences and photography adventures. Maybe I'll even get to write about my next trip, which I'm planning for November 2024.

That's a great idea, Ezra. I'm sure your readers will love hearing about your travels and seeing your photos. Don't forget to include some of your favorite hiking spots and woodworking projects in your blog.
